mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production directly captures manufacturing expansion and predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns because higher output supports corporate revenue growth.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between month-over-month INDPRO changes and 20-day S&P 500 returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 5.0% signals labor market deterioration that triggers recession-risk repricing and negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE crosses above 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of an elevated federal funds rate and contracting industrial production amplifies financial and real-economy stress, jointly producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term between FEDFUNDS level and INDPRO change exhibits a positive rank correlation with 20-day S&P 500 returns over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During housing-led expansions, a month-over-month decline in housing starts signals demand contraction that leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns following month-over-month HOUST declines are not significantly negative during housing-led expansions (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 5.0% restricts credit availability and raises equity discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales captures consumer spending momentum that drives revenue surprises and positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between month-over-month RSAFS changes and 5-day S&P 500 returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment combined with rising consumer prices compresses profit margins and equity valuations, jointly generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The bivariate interaction of UNRATE and CPIAUCSL changes shows a positive rank IC with 20-day S&P 500 returns over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth below 1.5% annualized signals demand stagnation that leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of quarters when GDPC1 growth is below 1.5% annualized.
